title: Extension manifest
description: Formal schema for the framework-core extension manifest contract per D29 (extension manifest contract + validation flow). The `extends` slot was removed per D31. Notation: JSON Schema Draft 2020-12 per D28. Layer-3 formal schema per D11. Cross-kind referential integrity (e.g., spec-ref resolution, identifier collision across extensions, conformance of referenced specs) is out of scope per D28; handled by the framework conformance validator (D30 + D32). Per D34 §2.4: the `vocabulary-slot` enum is intentionally closed at the six core open-vocabulary slots; adding a new open-vocabulary slot is a major bump on this contract per D33.

id.description: Stable extension identifier (D29 §1). Identifiers registered by an extension are implicitly namespaced by this id; canonical fully-qualified form is '<extension-id>:<vocabulary-identifier>' per D29.

vocabulary-registrations.description: List of (slot, identifier, spec-ref) tuples that add values to a core open-vocabulary slot (D29 §2). Required slot; may be [] (per D13 required-with-explicit-empty pattern).

payload-vocabulary-registrations.description: Optional. Per D59 §B.1 — registers open-vocab values for the four core payload-body slots (claim.confidence, action.action-name, state-change.what, lifecycle-transition.trigger). Parallel to vocabulary-registrations (which covers top-level slots); D59 extends D29's mechanism to payload-body values. Identifier namespacing per D29 (qualified form `<ext-id>:<value>`).

provisions.description: List of (kind, id, spec-ref) tuples — actual instances of kinds that this extension provides (D29 §3). Kinds that admit provisions: substrate, shape, adapter, specialist. (workspace, event, actor, work-unit are not extension-provided per D29.) Required slot; may be [].

dependencies.description: Dependencies the extension's provisions need to compose against (D29 §4). Both sub-slots are required; both may be [].

required-core-capabilities.description: Abstract core capabilities the provisions need (D17: hooks, skills, event-chain).

required-extensions.description: Other extensions this one depends on, by (id, version-range). Cycle detection + load order per D32; version-conflict resolution per D33.

spec-ref.description: Reference to a value's specification (typically a JSON Schema per D28). Treated as opaque-string-resolvable-by-the-loader per D29 — within-package paths, URLs, and registry references all admissible.

extension-reference.description: Reference to another extension by id + version range (used by 'dependencies.required-extensions[]').

vocabulary-slot.description: The set of framework-core open-vocabulary slots into which an extension may register a value, per D29 §2. Closed enum (per D34 §2.4); adding a new slot is a major bump per D33.

vocabulary-registration.description: A single (slot, identifier, spec-ref) tuple registering one value into one core open-vocabulary slot. The identifier is namespaced to the declaring extension per D29.

payload-vocabulary-registration.description: Per D59 §B.1 — a single (payload-slot, value, spec-ref?) tuple registering one open-vocab value into one core payload-body slot. spec-ref optional per D29 §2 pattern.

provision-kind.description: The set of kinds that admit extension-provided provisions, per D29 §3. Excludes workspace (per-deployment manifest) and event / actor / work-unit (runtime instances).

provision.description: A single (kind, id, spec-ref) tuple — one provision the extension provides. The provision conforms to its kind's layer-3 formal schema.
